channel.title: Preventative
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Preventative, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: ‘You Aren’t Trapped’: Hundreds of US Nurses Choose Canada Over Trump’s America
item.description: More than 1,000 American nurses have successfully applied for licensure in British Columbia since April, a massive increase over prior years. Ontario ...

item.title: ‘Kind of Morbid’: Health Premiums Threaten Their Nest Egg. A Terminal Diagnosis May Spare It.
item.description: Chaz and Jean Franklin were facing a sevenfold increase in their health premium payments with the expiration of enhanced federal subsidies for Afforda...

item.title: It's Time To Wash Your Makeup Brushes — Our Easy DIY Guide & Recipe
item.description: Be honest: When is the last time you washed your makeup brushes?

item.title: An Esthetician Shares How To Fix "Oily Dehydrated" Skin
item.description: Skin care concerns—and sometimes types—are never mutually exclusive.

item.title: This Spice Can Alleviate PMS: Here's How To Take It For Max Results
item.description: We spoke with a "spice expert" on how supplementation can curb symptoms of PMS.

item.title: Forget Biohacking — Here's What Longevity Really Looks Like for Women
item.description: Modern medicine has long treated women's health as a series of isolated chapters. Longevity care reframes the entire story.

item.title: The Results Are In & This Is The Dirtiest Spot On Most People's Desks
item.description: For those working from home, it's important to regularly clean your desk space.

item.title: Myasthenia Gravis Won’t Stop Me From Living My Best Life
item.description: February 28 is Rare Disease Day . As told to Jacquelyne Froeber When I was 6 years old, I was running around outside with my friends when I felt my bo...

item.title: En batalla por fondos, demócratas denuncian falta de atención médica para detenidos por el ICE
item.description: El esfuerzo de deportación masiva del presidente Donald Trump ha llevado a un número récord de inmigrantes detenidos en centros federales, cárceles lo...

item.title: Hypertension Bites Exposes a Silent Threat
item.description: In 2026, almost half of adults in this country are living with high blood pressure, according to the CDC, and many don’t notice it until it’s too late...

item.title: Jay Harris on Prostate Cancer
item.description: Jay Harris didn’t know how heavy his risk for prostate cancer was as a Black man before he was diagnosed. He thought his predicament was simply the re...

item.title: Human Braiding Hair May Be a Health Hazard Too
item.description: If you think switching to human hair for your protective styles is safer for your hair and health, think again. Consumer Reports followed up on the te...

item.title: Democrats Decry Meager Medical Care for Detainees in Funding Fight
item.description: A growing body of evidence indicates that immigrants in the custody of Immigration and Customs Enforcement face medical consequences because of seriou...

item.title: Will AI Solve Immunology’s Debate Over “Self vs. Non-Self?”
item.description: By MIKE MAGEE In 1872, English mathematician and sometimes poet, Augustus de Morgan, wrote this catching rhyme: “Great fleas have little fleas upon th...

item.title: Listen to the Latest ‘KFF Health News Minute’
item.description: The "KFF Health News Minute” brings original health care and health policy reporting from our newsroom to the airwaves each week.

item.title: Is Your Mental Health Putting Your Heart Health at Risk?
item.description: February is American Heart Month . A few weeks ago, as I was wrapping up my physical, my doctor asked, “When was the last time you had an echocardiogr...

item.description: English Febrero es el Mes del Corazón en Estados Unidos . Casi 1 de cada 2 adultos en Estados Unidos tiene hipertensión, pero solo la mitad de esas pe...

item.title: High Blood Pressure 101
item.description: Español February is American Heart Month . Almost 1 in 2 adults in the United States have high blood pressure, but only half of those people — about 1...

item.title: Can We Ride the GenAI Wave Without Getting Subsumed by It?
item.description: By DAVID SHAYWITZ “There are decades where nothing happens; and there are weeks where decades happen,” said Lenin, probably never. It’s also a remarka...

item.title: Are Protein Bars Actually Good for You?
item.description: Protein bars have become the go-to snack for busy women trying to eat healthier. They're tucked into gym bags, desk drawers and carry-on luggage, prom...

item.title: Reverend Jesse Jackson, a Civil Rights Legend, Has Passed Away
item.description: The Reverend Jesse Louis Jackson, Sr., who worked with Dr. Martin Luther King, founded the Rainbow PUSH Coalition and ran twice for the Democratic nom...

item.title: Sofia Noori, Nema Health
item.description: Nema Health has cracked the code on PTSD and has an amazing study showing that their technique of intensive cognitive processing therapy is over 99% e...

item.title: The Body Knows: Two Mothers Who Survived the Unthinkable
item.description: Motherhood is often framed as a new chapter, a deepening of identity, and the arrival of a love unlike any other. For two women, Anjellica Davis and E...

item.title: AI replaces me: The Private AI Bubble Meets the Public Digital Health Reality Check
item.description: By CLAUDE.AI trying to write like me MATTHEW HOLT I finally did what I have been wanting to do for a while and had an AI ingest everything I’ve writte...

item.title: Amodei Believes “AI-Enabled Autocracy” Could Be Imminent
